generated_oml:
// C2: Acting Components
instance CartMovementMotor : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Electromechanical motor responsible for horizontal translation of the gantry crane carriage with precise torque control for payload handling"
]

instance HoistMovementMotor : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Electromechanical motor controlling vertical elevation of the crane's hoist mechanism with position feedback for precise payload positioning"
]

instance CraneElectromagnet : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Electromagnetic actuator at the end of the hoist mechanism for puck (container) manipulation with on/off control for payload engagement"
]

// C3: Physical Sensing Components
instance SwingAngleEncoder : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "High-precision encoder measuring the crane's swing angle with millisecond resolution for closed-loop control validation"
    DTDFVocab:producedData SwingAngleData
]

instance CraneAnemometer : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Environmental sensor measuring airflow conditions affecting crane operations with real-time data transmission"
    DTDFVocab:producedData EnvironmentalData
]

instance CranePositionSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Position tracking sensor providing real-time crane kinematic state data including puck movement metrics"
    DTDFVocab:producedData CranePositionData, TrajectoryExecutionMetrics
]

// C4: Physical-to-Virtual Data Transmission
instance SwingAngleData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom SwingAngleEncoder
]

instance EnvironmentalData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom CraneAnemometer
]

instance CranePositionData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom CranePositionSensor
]

instance TrajectoryExecutionMetrics : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom CranePositionSensor
]

// C10: Models/Data
instance CraneKinematicModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Python-implemented custom kinematic model for trajectory planning and validation using Autodesk Fusion 360 geometric definitions"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo CraneSimulationEnabler
]

instance ContinuousValidationModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Custom distance metrics algorithm implemented in Python for comparing executed trajectories with simulated paths"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ValidationEnabler
]

instance CraneTrajectoryData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Real-time trajectory execution data including end-point residuals and swinging motion metrics"
    DTDFVocab:fromData TrajectoryExecutionMetrics
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ContinuousValidationService
]

instance OperationalMetricsDatabase : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "TimescaleDB time-series database storing crane operational metrics including position, velocity, torque, and energy consumption"
    DTDFVocab:fromData CranePositionData
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DataVisualizationEnabler
]

// C11: Enablers
instance CraneSimulationEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Python-based simulation environment using SciPy for kinematic simulations and trajectory planning"
    DTDFVocab:enables SimulationService
]

instance ValidationEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Custom Python implementation of continuous validation algorithms comparing physical measurements with digital twin predictions"
    DTDFVocab:enables ContinuousValidationService
]

instance DataVisualizationEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Grafana dashboard integration for real-time visualization of crane states and operational metrics"
    DTDFVocab:enables DataVisualizationService
]

instance MQTTBrokerEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "MQTT-based message broker facilitating real-time bidirectional data streams between physical crane and digital twin components"
    DTDFVocab:enables AllServices
]

instance DockerContainerization : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Docker-based containerization framework ensuring isolated deployment of digital twin services with reproducible environments"
    DTDFVocab:enables AllServices
]

// C6: Services
instance OptimalTrajectoryGenerationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service computing optimal crane motion trajectories using model predictive control principles with kinematic model inputs"
    DTDFVocab:provides OptimalTrajectoryInsight
]

instance ContinuousValidationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service continuously comparing physical trajectory execution with digital twin predictions using residual analysis"
    DTDFVocab:provides ValidationAlertAction
]

instance DataVisualizationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service providing 3D CAD model rendering and real-time dashboards for crane state visualization"
    DTDFVocab:provides CraneStateInsight
]

instance HistoricalDataLoggingService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service persisting operational data in TimescaleDB for post-mortem analysis and trend comparison"
    DTDFVocab:provides HistoricalAnalysisInsight
]

instance FaultDetectionService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service implementing machine learning-based anomaly detection for crane system failures"
    DTDFVocab:provides FaultDiagnosisInsight
]

// C17: Insights/Actions
instance OptimalTrajectoryInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Optimized motion trajectories with confidence intervals for energy-efficient crane operations"
]

instance ValidationAlertAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Automated alerts triggered when trajectory deviations exceed predefined thresholds"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance CraneStateInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Real-time visualization of crane kinematic states with live sensor data overlays"
]

instance HistoricalAnalysisInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Post-mortem analysis capabilities comparing historical operational trends with current performance"
]

instance FaultDiagnosisInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Root-cause analysis of system failures through correlation of sensor data with digital twin simulations"
]

// C18: Environments/Components
instance LabEnvironment : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:contains CranePhysicalSystem, DigitalTwinSystem
]

instance CranePhysicalSystem : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "Lab-scale gantry crane with mechanical structure, embedded controllers, and bidirectional interaction capabilities"
    base:isContainedIn LabEnvironment
]

instance DigitalTwinSystem : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "Containerized digital twin implementation with modular services for simulation, validation, and visualization"
    base:isContainedIn LabEnvironment
]

// C12: AllServices Definition
instance AllServices : DTDFVocab:Service [
    DTDFVocab:enables OptimalTrajectoryGenerationService, ContinuousValidationService,
                    DataVisualizationService, HistoricalDataLoggingService, FaultDetectionService
]

// C13: SimulationService Definition
instance SimulationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service providing crane simulation capabilities using the kinematic model"
    DTDFVocab:provides CraneStateInsight
]

// C19: System Under Study
instance CraneSystemUnderStudy : DTDFVocab:SystemUnderStudy [
    base:desc "Lab-scale gantry crane system for digital twin research and validation"
    DTDFVocab:hasEnvironment LabEnvironment
]

instance virtual_to_physical_interaction : DTDFVocab:VirtualToPhysical [
    base:desc "
The **virtual-to-physical interaction** in this system is facilitated by the **Control Application**, which generates and sends commands to the **Physical Crane Interface** to operate the gantry crane. Key components include:
- **Command Types**: Generated via the **Trajectory Generator Service**, which produces **optimal movement trajectories** for the crane, ensuring minimal residual swinging motion at the end of movements.
- **Control Message Formats**: Commands are transmitted as structured inputs to the **Control Application**, which translates them into control signals for the **Physical Crane Interface**. Exact message formats are unspecified but likely include kinematic parameters (e.g., target positions, velocities, accelerations).
- **Validation and Authentication Mechanisms**: No explicit validation/authentication protocols are mentioned for command transmission; however, the **Continuous Validation Service** ensures alignment between simulated and executed trajectories, indirectly validating command execution.
- **Feedback Systems**: The **Physical Crane Interface** likely provides feedback (e.g., sensor data, execution status) to the **Control Application** and **Logging Application** for closed-loop control and logging purposes.
- **Conflict Resolution**: Not explicitly detailed, but the **Continuous Validation Service** acts as a safeguard by comparing executed trajectories with simulations. If thresholds are breached (e.g., trajectory deviations), operators are notified via the **Dashboard** for manual intervention.
- **Command Execution Confirmation**: No explicit confirmation mechanism is described, but the **Logging Application** records executed trajectories, and the **Continuous Validation Service** validates their correctness post-execution.
"
]

instance dt_constellation : DTDFVocab:Constellation [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin systems in the documents employ an ad-hoc, non-commercial orchestration approach rather than relying on established digital twin frameworks. The GreenhouseDT exemplar uses a combination of **InfluxDB** for time-series data storage, **ActiveMQ** for message brokering, and **virtual machines** for deployment, facilitating behavioral adaptation of both the physical and digital components. The Air Quality Management exemplar integrates a **Raspberry Pi** with sensors for real-time data acquisition, an **AutomationML-based Digital Twin**, and **Microsoft Azure tools** for pipeline implementation, including runtime visualization, simulation, and prediction services. The Lab Scale Gantry Crane system, while not explicitly detailed in the provided documents, is implied to follow similar ad-hoc patterns based on the discussion of containerization and virtualization technologies. No centralized orchestration framework (e.g., Siemens MindSphere, PTC ThingWorx) is used; instead, the systems rely on modular, domain-specific architectures with explicit component integration (e.g., sensors ↔ Digital Twin ↔ cloud/edge services)."
]

instance life_cycle_stages : DTDFVocab:EvolutionStage [
    base:desc "
The lifecycle of the Digital Twin systems described in the documents is not explicitly detailed beyond general development and operational phases. However, the **GreenhouseDT** exemplar emphasizes **self-adaptive behavioral adaptation** of both the physical greenhouse system and its digital twin, implying iterative lifecycle stages where the twin evolves alongside the physical system. The **Air Quality Management** exemplar includes **runtime visualization, simulation, and prediction** applications, suggesting lifecycle phases such as:
  1. **Development Phase**: Initial setup with a Raspberry Pi-based sensor system and AutomationML-based digital twin model, leveraging Microsoft Azure tools for pipeline implementation.
  2. **Operational Phase**: Continuous runtime data collection, visualization, and predictive analytics for air quality management.
  3. **Adaptation Phase**: Implicit adaptation via model-driven engineering (MDE) approaches, though lifecycle governance (e.g., version control, compliance) is not explicitly documented.
The **Lab Scale Gantry Crane** system (mentioned in context) lacks lifecycle details but implies a **C4 Model-based architecture** for visualization and description, suggesting potential lifecycle stages could include:
  - **Design/Architecture Phase**: C4 Model diagrams for system context and container-level abstraction.
  - **Deployment Phase**: Physical system integration with digital twin components.
  No lifecycle governance frameworks (e.g., ISO/IEC standards) or transition management methodologies are referenced in the documents.
"
]

instance fidelity_and_validity_considerations : DTDFVocab:FidelityConsideration [
    base:desc "
The **Lab Scale Gantry Crane** system implements a **Continuous Validation Service** that **continuously compares measurements of executed trajectories** (from physical sensors) with **simulated trajectories** (from the digital twin). This service calculates **distance metrics** (e.g., Euclidean or kinematic error) and **compares results against an acceptable threshold**, triggering corrective actions if deviations exceed predefined limits. This mechanism ensures **model fidelity** by enforcing alignment between physical and digital representations. **Uncertainty quantification** is implicitly addressed via threshold-based validation, though explicit methods (e.g., Monte Carlo simulations) are not documented.

For **GreenhouseDT**, behavioral adaptation of the digital twin relies on **real-time data-driven adjustments**, but formal **validation and verification (V&V) methods** are not described. The **Air Quality Management** exemplar lacks explicit fidelity considerations, though its **simulation and prediction** applications imply reliance on **calibrated models** (e.g., AutomationML-based) for accuracy. Quality assurance processes are not detailed beyond the use of **commercial cloud tools (Microsoft Azure)** for pipeline implementation.
"
]

instance dt_technical_connection : DTDFVocab:TechnicalConnection [
    base:desc "
The **Digital Twin’s Technical Connection** infrastructure is centered around an **MQTT-based messaging system** for inter-service communication, with the **Physical Crane Interface** acting as the bridge between the physical and digital domains. Key details include:

- **Communication Protocols**:
  - **MQTT Broker**: Acts as the central hub for asynchronous messaging between digital twin services (e.g., **Trajectory Generator Service**, **Simulation Service**, **Logging Application**, **Dashboard**). MQTT topics are used to publish/subscribe to trajectory data, state updates, and validation alerts.
  - **Physical Crane Interface**: Uses unspecified protocols (e.g., serial, CAN bus) to communicate with the crane’s controllers, with no details on encryption or authentication.

- **Network Topology and Architecture**:
  - A **centralized architecture** with the **MQTT Broker** as the single point of inter-service communication. The **Physical Crane Interface** connects to the MQTT Broker to relay data between the crane and digital twin services. No mention of distributed or edge computing topologies.

- **Bandwidth and Latency Requirements**:
  - Not explicitly specified. However, the system implies **low-latency requirements** for real-time trajectory execution and validation, particularly for the **Continuous Validation Service** to compare simulated vs. executed trajectories promptly.

- **Reliability and Redundancy Mechanisms**:
  - No redundancy mechanisms (e.g., failover brokers, load balancing) are mentioned. The system relies on the **MQTT Broker’s persistence** (if configured) and **TimescaleDB’s durability** for data storage. Operator intervention via the **Dashboard** is required for recovery in case of failures.

- **Security Protocols**:
  - Not detailed. The **MQTT Broker** lacks explicit security protocols (e.g., TLS for encrypted communication, authentication for service-to-service communication). The **Physical Crane Interface** may use proprietary security measures for crane controller communication, but these are unspecified.
"
]

instance dt_hosting_deployment : DTDFVocab:Deployment [
    base:desc "
The **Digital Twin’s Hosting and Deployment** infrastructure is **on-premise**, co-located with the **Physical Crane**, and follows a **microservices architecture** for digital twin components while maintaining a **monolithic deployment model** for physical hardware. Key details include:

- **Hosting Platforms**:
  - **On-premise**: All components (physical crane, digital twin services, and database) are deployed locally, with no references to cloud (e.g., AWS, Azure) or edge computing platforms.

- **Computational and Storage Requirements**:
  - Not explicitly quantified. However, the system requires:
    - **Database (TimescaleDB)**: Optimized for time-series data storage (e.g., trajectory logs, sensor readings).
    - **MQTT Broker**: Moderate computational resources for handling asynchronous messaging between services.
    - **Simulation Service**: Resource-intensive for real-time kinematic simulations, though specifics (e.g., CPU cores, GPU requirements) are not provided.
    - **Trajectory Generator Service**: Lightweight compared to simulation, but requires access to the kinematics model.

- **Scalability Mechanisms**:
  - Not detailed. The deployment is containerized using **Docker** for the **Database**, **MQTT Broker**, and **Dashboard**, enabling modular scaling. However, no mention of auto-scaling (e.g., Kubernetes), horizontal pod scaling, or vertical scaling strategies.

- **Deployment Models Supported**:
  - **Microservices**: Digital twin services (e.g., **Trajectory Generator**, **Simulation Service**, **Continuous Validation Service**) are deployed as independent containers.
  - **Monolithic**: The **Physical Crane** operates as a standalone hardware system without digital twin dependencies.
  - **Container Orchestration**: Docker is used for containerization, but no orchestration tools (e.g., Kubernetes) are referenced.

- **Availability/Reliability Assurance**:
  - Relies on:
    - **TimescaleDB’s durability** for data persistence.
    - **MQTT Broker’s persistence** (if enabled) for message queuing.
    - **Human-in-the-loop validation** via the **Dashboard** for operator intervention in case of failures (e.g., trajectory deviations, service crashes).
  - No redundancy or high-availability mechanisms (e.g., multi-node deployments, backup brokers) are mentioned.
"
]

instance horizontal_integration : DTDFVocab:HoriIntegration [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin systems exhibit limited horizontal integration with external systems. The GreenhouseDT exemplar integrates with **InfluxDB** for data storage and **ActiveMQ** for messaging, but no explicit interoperability standards (e.g., OPC UA, MQTT) or enterprise system dependencies are documented. The Air Quality Management exemplar leverages **Microsoft Azure** for cloud-based services but does not detail integration with external Digital Twins or enterprise systems beyond its own pipeline. The Lab Scale Gantry Crane system is not described in sufficient detail to infer horizontal integration patterns. Overall, the exemplars prioritize standalone functionality over cross-system interoperability, with no mention of standardized protocols or dependencies on external Digital Twin ecosystems."
]

instance standardization : DTDFVocab:Standardization [
    base:desc "
The Digital Twin system described in the documents **does not explicitly comply with formal industry standards**, relying instead on **ad-hoc integration of open-source technologies**. The **GreenhouseDT** exemplar uses **InfluxDB** (time-series database) and **ActiveMQ** (message broker), both non-standardized for digital twins but widely adopted in IoT and real-time systems. The **Air Quality Management** exemplar employs **AutomationML** (a standardized data exchange format for automation systems, per ISO 23247), though the document does not cite the ISO standard number explicitly. Microsoft Azure tools are used for cloud-based pipeline implementation, but no compliance frameworks (e.g., ISO/IEC 42010 for digital twin standards) are referenced.

Interoperability is achieved through **Raspberry Pi sensor integration** and **AutomationML-based twin representation**, but no **API standards** (e.g., OPC UA) or **data exchange protocols** (e.g., MQTT with standardized topics) are mandated. The **Lab Scale Gantry Crane** system does not mention any standardization efforts, relying on **C4 Model documentation** for architecture clarity rather than compliance with digital twin frameworks.

Certification requirements are **not addressed**, and the system’s adherence to **industry specifications** (e.g., IEC 62264 for enterprise integration) is implicit rather than explicit. The overall approach is **non-compliant with formal digital twin standards**, instead favoring **flexible, domain-specific toolchains** (e.g., Docker, GitHub) for dissemination.
"
]
